title: Diphasium madeirense
taxonomy: Diphasium madeirense is classified as a species of fern in the family Lycopodiaceae. This family is a group of ancient vascular plants known as club mosses. Diphasium madeirense belongs to the genus Diphasium, which is a genus of lycopods. The species is native to the island of Madeira, Portugal. It is a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation.

intro: Diphasium madeirense, also known as Madeira club moss, is a species of vascular plant in the family Lycopodiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be beneficial for wound healing and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Diphasium madeirense include sesquiterpenoids, diterpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Diphasium madeirense is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal remedies such as tinctures and decoctions.
